ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for martabak manis (Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake). FOODON_00002683 (pancake food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Pancakes) used as closest parent. No specific code for martabak manis with pandan, cheese, and milk filling exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for martabak manis or Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Pandan Cheese Milk Martabak is 3 pieces, weighing approximately 150 g, as typically portioned at Indonesian martabak stalls and delivery outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Pancake, plain, prepared from recipe — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18259); adapted with dairy and egg filling components from USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods for cheese and whole milk
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for martabak manis pandan cheese milk exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted calculation: plain pancake batter base (USDA SR Legacy NDB 18259) combined with proportional contributions from whole milk (USDA FDC 01077), cheddar cheese (USDA FDC 01009), egg (USDA FDC 01123), butter (USDA FDC 01001), and sugar. Pandan extract contributes negligible macronutrients. The 'less sweet' descriptor from the input record is reflected in a moderately reduced sugar estimate relative to standard martabak manis. Values represent a per-100g composite estimate for the finished product.

gi_source.title: Pancake, made from mix — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for martabak manis pandan cheese milk. GI of 67 is adopted from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for pancake made from mix (GI ~67, medium category; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). Martabak manis shares a comparable batter composition (refined wheat flour, eggs, milk, sugar, leavening) and cooking method (pan-fried). The cheese and milk filling add fat and protein which may modestly lower the effective GI, but the dominant carbohydrate matrix from refined flour justifies retention of the medium GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (67 × 57.75) / 100 ≈ 38.6, where carbohydrate per 150g serving = 38.5 × 1.5 = 57.75g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~280 mg per 100g; ~420 mg per 3-piece serving) from cheese and added salt in batter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit portion size to 1–2 pieces per occasion, as the sodium contribution from cheese and batter salt can approach 20% of the 2000 mg daily sodium target in a single 3-piece serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (~38.6) driven by refined wheat flour batter and added sugar, despite the 'less sweet' formulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional food only; the medium GI (67) combined with a large carbohydrate load per serving (~58g per 3 pieces) produces a high glycemic load that will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation, and portion should be restricted to 1 piece with a protein-rich accompaniment to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~6.4g per 100g; ~9.6g per serving) from butter, cheese, and whole milk, combined with cholesterol (~85 mg per 100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this food due to its saturated fat and cholesterol content from dairy and egg ingredients; a 3-piece serving provides approximately 48% of the recommended daily saturated fat limit of 20g, and should be consumed infrequently as part of a diet otherwise low in saturated fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pandan Cheese Milk Martabak (Martabak Manis Pandan Keju Susu) is an Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake flavoured with pandan (screwpine leaf extract) and filled with a mixture of cheese and sweetened condensed or fresh milk. It belongs to the martabak manis family — thick, spongy, pan-fried pancakes made from a leavened batter of wheat flour, eggs, milk, sugar, butter, and baking powder or yeast. The pandan extract gives the batter a distinctive green hue and a fragrant, vanilla-like aroma characteristic of Southeast Asian desserts. After cooking, the pancake is folded in half over a generous filling of grated cheese and milk, then sliced into portions. This version is described as 'less sweet', indicating a reduced sugar formulation compared to the standard martabak manis. A 3-piece serving (approximately 150g) provides roughly 428 kcal, 58g of carbohydrate, 17g of fat, and 11g of protein. The glycemic load per serving is high (~38.6), making it a significant postprandial glucose challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The cheese and butter content contribute meaningful saturated fat (~9.6g per serving) and sodium (~420mg per serving), which are relevant for cardiovascular disease and hypertension management. Martabak manis is widely sold at Indonesian street food stalls and has become popular in Singapore through Indonesian restaurant chains and food delivery platforms. It is typically consumed as a dessert or snack, often shared among multiple people. The food is not suitable for vegans or those with gluten, dairy, or egg allergies.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for martabak manis pandan cheese milk was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted calculation from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for plain pancake, whole milk, cheddar cheese, egg, and butter. GI is adopted from the closest analogous food (pancake from mix, GI 67) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Serving size of 150g (3 pieces) is estimated based on typical martabak manis portion sizes at Indonesian food stalls in Singapore; actual weight per piece varies by vendor. 2. The 'less sweet' descriptor from the input record is reflected in a sugar estimate of 14g per 100g, which is lower than the ~18-22g per 100g typical of standard martabak manis. 3. Pandan extract contributes negligible macronutrients and is not separately itemised in nutrient calculations. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component data and are not directly reported for this specific product; these values carry higher uncertainty than the core macros. 5. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record; all micronutrient fields are populated with component-based estimates from USDA FoodData Central. 6. Food group is retained as 'Dairy and eggs' per the input record, reflecting the primary filling ingredients (cheese and milk); an alternative classification of 'Desserts' or 'Snacks' would also be defensible. 7. Halal status is assessed as likely given the Indonesian Muslim food culture context and the absence of pork or alcohol ingredients, but has not been independently verified for the specific Smile Martabak - Funan outlet.
